title: [Feature]: Add explicit JetStream consumer creation and reconciliation
problem: The runner can bind to or create a durable consumer, but production operators often need explicit reconciliation behavior. They need to know whether configuration drift is detected, rejected, or updated, especially for consumer properties that affect redelivery, ACK timing, and backpressure.
proposal: Add a clear consumer management mode that can bind-only, create-if-missing, or reconcile according to documented rules. The runner validates expected stream, subject filter, durable name, explicit ACK policy, pull-consumer shape, headers-only mode when configured, and delivery-related settings. Unsafe drift fails closed with a readable error rather than silently changing message delivery behavior.
users: Operators managing JetStream consumers across environments, maintainers testing NATS compatibility, and release engineers documenting production setup.
delivery_semantics: Consumer reconciliation is delivery-sensitive. The feature must preserve pull-based processing, explicit ACK after durable success, and bounded redelivery behavior. Drift that could weaken commit-then-ACK must be rejected or require explicit opt-in.
security: Consumer management may require broader NATS permissions than bind-only operation. Documentation must separate runtime least privilege from administrative reconciliation permissions and avoid embedding private account details.
acceptance: Consumer management modes are explicit and documented.
Unsafe drift fails closed with human-readable errors.
Tests cover bind-only, create-if-missing, compatible existing consumer, and incompatible drift.
No existing default behavior becomes less safe.
tests: Unit tests cover bind-only, create-if-missing, compatible existing consumer, incompatible drift, reconcile behavior, runner startup ordering, configuration validation, and public API compatibility. Optional integration tests against local NATS remain future certification evidence. Run scripts/check.sh and relevant e2e tests.
documentation: Update docs/configuration.md, docs/architecture.md, docs/operations.md, docs/security.md, docs/nats-feature-gap-analysis.md, ROADMAP.md, and CHANGELOG.md.
closeout: Close after release publication. Include release tag, modes added, tests run, permission notes, headers-only compatibility notes, and limitations.
